scriptText:
I. NEXT YEAR

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic scene featuring @ROB as ROBERT, a terminally ill father, and @EVAN as EVAN, his son, in a dramatic grief-themed story.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain strict spatial continuity around the central chopping block. Keep the 180-degree axis locked on the left-to-right eye-line. Track the heavy pine log as a shared physical and emotional anchor across cuts.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Melancholic realism, 35mm film texture, anamorphic widescreen.
Palette: Slate gray, moss green, deep brown, mustard yellow.
Lighting: Overcast cold daylight, soft directional rim light.
Location: Weathered cedar-plank cabin in damp pine woods.
Robert: Pale skin, sunken eyes, messy gray-curly hair; wearing a distressed brown oilskin jacket, faded denim shirt, work gloves.
Evan: Neatly styled wavy hair, light stubble; wearing a mustard-yellow knit sweater, dark work pants.
Audio: No music. Foley/ambient only: wind, heavy ragged breathing, scraping bark, distant crows.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
Establishing WS of a weathered cedar-plank cabin enveloped in misty pine woods, a rustic chopping block standing prominent in the damp center-ground.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] Low angle, slow push -> [BLOCK] Robert stands frame-left over the chopping block, Evan stands frame-right -> [ACT] Robert struggles to hoist a massive pine log, his shoulders trembling -> [ENV] Cold mist rising from the wet ground.
[CAM] OTS tracking shot over Robert’s shoulder -> [ACT] Evan steps forward, hands open and welcoming -> [AUDIO] Evan: "Let me, Dad."
[CAM] ECU, shallow depth of field -> [STATE IN] Robert's hands in worn leather gloves -> [ACT] Robert's gloved fingers dig violently into the rough, wet bark -> [AUDIO] Evan (O.S.): "You've been at it for hours."
[CAM] MCU, low-angle on Robert -> [STATE IN] Sweat on Robert's pale brow, labored breath -> [ACT] Robert glares downward, gripping the log tight to his chest -> [AUDIO] Robert: "If you don't stack it right, the bottom rots."
[CAM] Tight CU on Evan -> [STATE IN] Evan's soft eyes hardening -> [ACT] Evan swallows hard, jaw tightening in a silent moment of realization -> [AUDIO] Robert (O.S.): "I won't be here next year."
[CAM] MCU, profile shot holding both in frame -> [ACT] Evan steps closer but keeps his hands at his sides, refusing to touch the wood -> [AUDIO] Evan: "Then teach me."
[CAM] Wide shot from ground level -> [ACT] Robert stares at the log, his shoulders slumping as he slowly lets it drop to the damp earth with a heavy thud -> [AUDIO] Robert: "I'm trying."
[CAM] Tilt-down close shot -> [STATE IN] The log resting in wet pine needles -> [ACT] The wind gently rustles the dry leaves around the wood -> [AUDIO] Cold wind gust, distant crow, absolute silence from the characters.
[</BRIEF>]

II. NO SAFE PLACE

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a dramatic scene featuring @SARAH as Sarah (the terminally ill mother), @LEA as Lea (the protective elder daughter), and @ROSA as Rosa (the frantic younger daughter) fleeing a city clinic.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain a strict 180-degree axis line on the sidewalk, keeping Sarah frame-right and Lea clutching her right arm, with Rosa positioned frame-left and slightly behind. Ensure match-on-action tracking shots that preserve their continuous, fast-paced forward momentum. 
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Gritty realist drama, shallow depth of field, high-contrast anamorphic look.
Location: Rain-slicked downtown Chicago sidewalk at cold autumn dusk.
Lighting: Cool blue twilight ambient light contrasted with harsh amber and orange neon flares from shop windows and warm passing headlights.
Sound: No music. Pure ambient foley: roaring rush-hour traffic, wet tires hissing on asphalt, wind whistling, heavy boots clicking frantically on concrete, and raw, breathless vocal tracks.
Sarah Styling: Beige wool trench coat clutched tight at the collar, damp straight blonde-grey hair, pale face with red tear-stained eyes, no makeup.
Lea Styling: Heavy dark-olive puffer jacket, curly black hair pulled back into a messy high bun, tense brow, natural makeup.
Rosa Styling: Oversized black leather jacket over a dark blue knit scarf, chin-length dark bob with distinct yellow-dyed streaks near the front, flushed cheeks, wet eyes.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
A composed, tracking MCU frame moving backward at eye level, capturing Sarah walking rapidly toward the camera-left of the axis, her face devastated. Lea walks tightly on her right, hand locked around Sarah's elbow. Rosa is positioned half a step behind on the left.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MCU, tracking backward ahead of Sarah, keeping her centered -> [ACT] Sarah walks fast, hands clutching her beige woolen coat tight to her chest. Lea maintains a protective grip on Sarah’s arm. Rosa steps up on the left side, eyes wild with panic -> [AUDIO] Traffic roar, wet footsteps. Rosa (breathless, catching up): "We can't just... accept that. There have to be other trials."
[CAM] OTS MCU over Sarah's left shoulder, looking back slightly at Lea and Rosa walking in lockstep -> [ACT] Lea looks sharply at Rosa, shaking her head with deep urgency, her grip tightening on Sarah’s arm. Rosa looks back desperately -> [AUDIO] Rain hissing on asphalt. Lea (low, urgent): "Rosa, stop. Not here. Not on the street."
[CAM] MS side tracking shot, profiling all three moving left-to-right past glowing neon shop windows -> [ACT] Sarah keeps staring straight ahead with glazed, wet eyes, her jaw clenched. Rosa lunges slightly forward to catch Sarah's eye, gesturing with her hand -> [AUDIO] Distant police siren. Sarah (voice cracking): "I just... I want to get to the car." Rosa (frantic, voice rising): "Mom, please, look at me—"
[CAM] MCU tracking backward, centered on Sarah's face -> [ACT] Sarah ignores the plea, staring blankly into the cold air as a tear spills down her cheek. Lea keeps her head down, walking forward firmly. Rosa slowly falls half a step back, her hand dropping in defeat -> [AUDIO] Heavy whoosh of a passing city bus, wet tire spray, three pairs of boots hitting concrete, rapid breathing. No dialogue.
[</BRIEF>]

III. OUT OF SYNC

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a dramatic, emotionally tense scene featuring @ROB as ROBERT, an aging, retired audio engineer, and @JOHN as JOHN, his estranged adult son.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Ensure absolute spatial continuity on a 180-degree axis with Robert on frame-left and John on frame-right. Maintain consistent dampness and water-damage details across all angles. Track the physical state of the reel-to-reel tape machine and keep the dust-mote atmosphere consistent across cuts.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: 35mm grit, analog realism
Lighting: Warm amber console glow contrasting harsh, cold blue dusk window light
Palette: Rust orange, mold green, slate grey
Location: Flooded, decaying basement mixing room, peeling acoustic foam, wet concrete floor
Wardrobe: Robert wearing a grease-stained beige work shirt; John wearing a damp, dark green corduroy jacket over a grey tee
Hair/Makeup: Robert has wild, unkempt silver curls and damp sweat on brow; John has limp, wet-look light-brown hair tucked behind ears, eyes red-rimmed
Sound: Diegetic only. Low tape hum, physical clicks, console feedback, distant water drips
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
MCU, low angle, looking up at an old reel-to-reel tape deck resting on a water-damaged wooden shelf. The left metal reel is spinning slowly, emitting a low-frequency hum under amber lighting. Dust motes float in the damp air.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] CU, slow DOLLY IN on spinning tape reel -> [ACT] Dust particles kick off the rotating magnetic ribbon as a distorted, low female voice speaks through static -> [AUDIO] Tape hiss, muffled female voice: "...we had that summer..."
[CAM] MS, TILT UP from a stack of moldy tape boxes to John standing frame-right, staring down -> [ACT] John rubs his temples with his right hand, his shoulders tense and hunched, breathing heavily -> [AUDIO] John: "That wasn't July, Dad. She was already too sick."
[CAM] OTS over John's shoulder to Robert at the console, frame-left -> [ACT] Robert keeps his left hand resting on a fader, adjusting it sharply, eyes fixed on the VU meter needle leaping into the red -> [AUDIO] Robert: "I mixed this session myself. I know the date, John."
[CAM] MCU, PAN L to R following John as he steps closer to the console -> [ACT] John shakes his head, teeth clenched, pointing a finger at the spinning reel -> [AUDIO] John: "You weren't even there! You were in Chicago!"
[CAM] CU, tight on Robert, warm amber light hitting his weathered cheek -> [ACT] Robert flinches, his jaw tightening, eyes glistening with sudden tears as he glares at John off-screen -> [AUDIO] Robert: "Don't you dare tell me where I was."
[CAM] OTS over Robert's shoulder focusing on John, frame-right -> [ACT] John's defensive posture crumbles; his jaw slackens and he lets out a shaky exhale -> [AUDIO] John: "We both left her alone."
[CAM] MS, wide tracking shot circling slowly around both men standing side-by-side -> [ACT] Robert slowly takes his hand off the console slider, letting his arm drop to his side, staring blankly ahead -> [AUDIO] Tape hiss continues, mother's distorted voice (O.S.): "...always together..."
[CAM] CU on John's hand slowly reaching out and gripping the cold metal edge of the mixing board -> [ACT] His knuckles turn white as he suppresses a sob, head bowed, long hair falling forward -> [AUDIO] John (O.S.): (heavy shaky sigh)
[CAM] ECU, locked-off shot of the tape reel suddenly stopping -> [ACT] The magnetic tape ends and flaps loosely against the plastic spindle, spinning freely in the dim amber light -> [AUDIO] Steady, monotonous click-click of the loose tape, background dripping water
[</BRIEF>]

IV. STRANGERS AGAIN

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic scene featuring @LEA as LEA and @EVAN as EVAN. Emotion: Restrained grief, quiet heartbreak.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Ensure absolute axis discipline along the 180-degree line of the narrow pharmacy aisle. Maintain spatial tracking of the medicine box in Lea's trembling hands and the paper receipt in Evan's left hand. Prioritize subtle, breathing handheld camera movement over smooth dolly tracks to emphasize raw, unpolished performance beats.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Medium: anamorphic lenses, high-contrast cinematic realism, shallow depth of field.
Palette: Desaturated clinical whites, muted teals, cold grays, accented by the heavy dark winter coats.
Lighting: Harsh overhead fluorescent tubes casting sterile white light, creating soft dark shadows under the eyes.
Location: A cramped, cluttered aisle of a local coastal Maine pharmacy, shelves stocked with clinical medicine boxes.
Wardrobe (Lea): Heavy olive-drab winter parka over a thick, charcoal-gray wool cable-knit sweater.
Wardrobe (Evan): Weathered, faded navy canvas work jacket over a dark-patterned flannel shirt.
Sound: No music. Ambient only. Low fluorescent hum, squeak of boots on linoleum, paper receipt crinkling, shaky breathing.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
[ENV INSERT] Extreme close-up of a crumpled grocery receipt being tightly squeezed in a man's chapped, trembling left hand, the harsh sterile white overhead light reflecting off the shiny thermal paper.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MCU, low-angle looking up slightly at Evan, frame-right -> [ACT] Evan stands frozen, clutching the receipt in his left hand, his eyes wide and glassy with unshed tears as his jaw tenses -> [AUDIO] EVAN: (voice cracking) "Hey. You... you look good, Lea."
[CAM] OTS over Evan's shoulder, tight on Lea frame-left -> [ACT] Lea fumbles a small white box of cold medicine in her trembling fingers, her eyes downcast before darting up to meet his -> [AUDIO] LEA: (voice high and tight) "Thanks. I’m just... getting some things."
[CAM] CU, side profile, shallow depth of field focusing on Evan's twitching right hand -> [ACT] Evan steps forward slightly into the light, his empty right hand twitching in a half-gesture toward her before dropping -> [AUDIO] EVAN: (breathless) "I wanted to... I never called because—"
[CAM] MCU, low-angle on Lea, camera-left of axis -> [ACT] Lea shakes her head frantically, her lips trembling as she clutches the medicine box tightly to her chest and takes a step back -> [AUDIO] LEA: (desperate, cutting him off) "Don't. Please. There's nothing... we don't have to."
[CAM] ECU, tight on Evan's eyes, glistening in the harsh white light -> [ACT] Evan's gaze drops slightly, his throat muscles moving as he swallows, his expression utterly defeated -> [AUDIO] EVAN: (whispering) "My heart is broken, Lea. It's still..."
[CAM] CU on Lea, side profile -> [ACT] Lea looks away toward the cold metal shelf, biting her bottom lip hard to suppress a sob, a single tear escaping her eye -> [AUDIO] Shaky, shallow inhale, distant hum of the pharmacy refrigerator.
[CAM] Wide-angle, low down on the dirty linoleum floor, looking down the narrow aisle -> [ACT] Lea takes a hurried, shaky step past Evan, her heavy boots squeaking on the floor, leaving him standing motionless -> [AUDIO] Squeak of boots on linoleum, rustle of her heavy winter parka.
[CAM] MCU, OTS from behind Lea's retreating, shaking shoulders -> [ACT] Evan remains frozen in the center of the frame, bathed in the dead-white fluorescent light, watching her walk away into the distance without turning back -> [AUDIO] Evan's heavy, trembling exhale, overhead fluorescent light hum rising slightly.
[</BRIEF>]

V. WHAT REMAINS

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a neorealist indie drama scene featuring @ROB as Robert (an aging, weathered father) and @JOHN as John (his intense, estranged son).
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain spatial continuity in a dusty, half-packed room. Keep prop tracking consistent with a cardboard box of vinyl records. Trace Robert's transition from anger to vacant confusion.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Medium: 35mm film, naturalistic grain
Palette: Desaturated amber, dusty ochres, soft shadows
Lighting: Direct golden afternoon light casting long window-pane shadows
Location: Cluttered, mid-century suburban living room with stacked moving boxes
Hair/Makeup: Robert (disheveled curly grey hair, weathered skin); John (messy shoulder-length dirty-blond hair, sweat-sheen)
Wardrobe: Robert wearing faded olive knit polo; John wearing dusty dark-grey denim shirt over a white tee
Audio: Diegetic only. Cardboard scraping, heavy breathing, floorboard creaks, distant cicadas
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
MS, handheld, facing John standing frame-left, clutching a cardboard box of vinyl records tightly against his chest, glaring off-frame right at Robert near a sunlit window.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MS, handheld, drift-left -> [ACT] John grips the box, knuckles white -> John: "We can't keep everything, Dad. Just pick." -> [AUDIO] Cardboard scraping.
[CAM] MCU, low-angle on Robert near window -> [ACT] Robert points an accusatory finger, chest heaving -> Robert: "That's my life in those boxes!" -> [AUDIO] Cicadas buzzing.
[CAM] OTS behind Robert looking at John -> [ACT] John steps closer, jaw tight with resentment -> John: "You didn't care about this house for ten years!" -> [AUDIO] Heavy breathing.
[CAM] CU, tracking Robert -> [ACT] Robert opens his mouth to yell, but freezes. His pointing hand stays suspended, his angry gaze suddenly shifting to a hollow, terrifying blankness -> [AUDIO] Floorboard creak.
[CAM] INS, close-up on the box -> [ACT] John's grip on the cardboard box loosens, a vinyl sleeve sliding outward slightly -> [AUDIO] Paper rustling.
[CAM] MCU, OTS behind Robert's frozen shoulder -> [ACT] John's anger instantly drains, replaced by suffocating shock, his lips parting -> John: "Dad...?" -> [AUDIO] Sudden dead silence.
[CAM] CU on Robert -> [ACT] Robert's hand slowly drops to his side. He blinks rapidly, looking around the room as if seeing it for the first time -> Robert: "Where... where is your mother?" -> [AUDIO] Clock ticking faintly.
[CAM] MS, profile tracking -> [ACT] John slowly, silently lowers the heavy box onto a wooden table frame-left, keeping his eyes locked on his father -> John: "She's not here, Dad." -> [AUDIO] Dull thud of cardboard on wood.
[CAM] WS, extreme low-angle -> [ACT] Thick dust motes drift lazily through a bright shaft of sunlight cutting between the two men -> [AUDIO] Distant wind.
[CAM] MCU, slow push-in on John -> [ACT] John's eyes well with tears. He swallows hard, staring at his father's vacant silhouette in the foreground -> [AUDIO] Shaky sigh, silence.
[</BRIEF>]

VI. SPOTLESS

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic scene featuring @SARAH as SARAH and @JOHN as JOHN in a gritty, emotionally raw family drama style.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain a strict 180-degree axis across the diagonal of the taut bedsheet. Ensure physical match-on-action as the sheet changes tension. Restate that the sheet remains white and clean until it falls. Prioritize micro-expressions of repressed grief over theatrical gestures.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: 1980s American suburban realism; tactile, melancholic, intimate.
Location: Cramped basement laundry room with exposed copper pipes and wood-paneled walls.
Lighting: Single source of warm, dusty late-afternoon sun filtering through a high, small window, clashing with the sterile cool light of an overhead fluorescent fixture.
Color Palette: Faded mustard yellows, muted browns, steel greys, contrasted against a bright, sterile white bedsheet.
Sarah Styling: Weary look, minimal makeup highlighting faint under-eye circles. Blonde hair pulled back into a tight, slightly messy low bun. Wearing an oversized, faded pale yellow knit cardigan over a plain white linen button-down.
John Styling: Disheveled, wavy dirty blonde hair pushed back behind his ears. Natural, un-styled appearance with visible jaw tension. Wearing a faded olive-green thermal henley with sleeves pushed up to his mid-forearms.
Sound Design: No music. Heavy, rhythmic rumbling and metallic vibration of an active clothes dryer, the crisp rustling of heavy cotton linen, and shaky, shallow breathing.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
Inside a cramped, dimly lit basement laundry room, an extreme close-up focuses on the lint trap of a vintage dryer, hot air visibly shimmering as lavender-scented steam escapes into a shaft of dusty sunlight.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] Low-angle, wide shot looking up from floor-level. The frame is horizontally bisected by a large, taut white bedsheet held tight -> [ACT] Sarah stands frame-left, eyes locked forward with intense focus. John stands frame-right, holding his end with a white-knuckled grip -> [AUDIO] Deep dryer rumble, sharp rustle of fabric. -> Sarah: "Don't drag the corner on the floor, John."
[CAM] High-angle close-up looking down at John's hands gripping the corners of the sheet -> [ACT] John sighs heavily, rolling his eyes as he slightly drops his wrists, loosening his side of the fabric -> [AUDIO] John's heavy sigh, dry friction of cotton. -> John: "It’s clean, Mom. It doesn't matter."
[CAM] Extreme close-up of Sarah’s face in profile, her jaw tightening as she stares forward -> [ACT] She pulls her end of the sheet back sharply to restore the tension -> [AUDIO] High-pitched creak of the wooden floorboards under her feet. -> Sarah: "It matters to me..." -> Sarah (O.S.): "...We keep things clean."
[CAM] Medium close-up, OTS looking past Sarah's shoulder at John -> [ACT] John's eyes narrow in sudden, bitter anger. He jerks his arms back hard, snapping the sheet taut with a violent pop -> [AUDIO] Loud, whip-like snap of the bedsheet. -> John: "Is that why you cleared out his closet already? To keep things 'clean'?"
[CAM] Close-up on Sarah's face -> [ACT] Her eyes widen in shock. Her lips part slightly as she gasps, her fingers instantly losing their grip and letting go of the fabric -> [AUDIO] Short, sharp intake of breath. -> Sarah: "That’s... that is not fair."
[</BRIEF>]

VII. SENTIMENTAL VALUE

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic realism drama scene featuring @ROB as Robert (the estranged, grieving father) and @JOHN as John (the resentful, tense son) clashing in a dusty, cluttered garage.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain a strict 180-degree axis between the workbench (frame-left) and the cardboard boxes (frame-right). Ensure the rusted socket wrench remains in Robert's right hand and the tape dispenser in John's hands. Use matching lighting directions across cuts to preserve spatial continuity.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Medium: 35mm anamorphic film, heavy grain, shallow depth of field.
Palette: Desaturated amber, cold steel grey, deep shadow blues.
Lighting: High-contrast chiaroscuro; dramatic shafts of morning light cutting through dust motes.
Wardrobe: Robert wears a faded grey tee under an unbuttoned olive-drab work jacket. John wears a dark charcoal flannel over a white Henley.
Styling: Robert has messy, silver-streaked curly hair and weathered skin. John has shoulder-length wavy blonde-brown hair tied in a loose half-bun, with tired, dark-rimmed eyes.
Sound: No Music. Diegetic Only. A low, heavy cello drone layered beneath the harsh, diegetic sounds of tape tearing and metallic clinks.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
Low-angle extreme close-up of a rusted 1970s socket wrench being slowly turned over by calloused, grease-stained fingers, back-lit by a sharp ray of dusty amber light.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] Dutch-tilt ECU on the rusted socket wrench -> [ACT] Robert’s thumb slowly strokes the cold metal -> [AUDIO] Low ambient hum, faint metallic scraping.
[CAM] Low-angle OTS looking past Robert's shoulder toward John in the background -> [ACT] Robert stands rigid at the cluttered wooden workbench. John is hunched over a pile of cardboard boxes, back turned -> [AUDIO] Robert: "Your grandfather bought this in '72. Still works."
[CAM] High-angle extreme close-up on John's hands -> [ACT] John violently pulls a strip of brown packing tape off a squealing dispenser -> [AUDIO] Sharp, piercing screech of tearing tape. John (O.S.): "It’s trash, Dad."
[CAM] Profile MCU of John, camera tracking slightly as he works -> [ACT] John keeps his head down, jaw clenched, avoiding eye contact as he smooths the tape with a hard swipe -> [AUDIO] John: "Let it go."
[CAM] Low-angle CU on Robert's face, dusty light highlighting his weathered features -> [ACT] Robert's chin trembles slightly, his gaze fixed on the wrench in his right hand -> [AUDIO] Robert: "Some things..."
[CAM] ECU on the tape dispenser -> [ACT] John's hands suddenly freeze mid-motion, his fingers squeezing the plastic handle until knuckles turn white -> [AUDIO] Sudden, heavy silence. Robert (O.S.): "...they don't lose value just because they're old, John."
[CAM] Extreme low-angle OTS looking up from the box floor to John's face -> [ACT] John slowly tilts his head up, eyes cold, staring intensely across the garage at his father -> [AUDIO] John: "Then you should have kept better care of them."
[</BRIEF>]

VIII. IN THE RED

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a indie drama scene featuring @JOHN as John, a volatile, guilt-ridden head chef, and @ROSA as Rosa, his rebellious sister-in-law, clashing in a closed kitchen over their partner's tragic overdose.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain spatial continuity across the central kitchen island axis. Keep Rosa screen-left and John screen-right. Track the black clipboard prop across cuts. Ensure match-on-action during physical outbursts.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Raw indie realism, gritty, high-contrast handheld look.
Lighting: Harsh, flickering overhead fluorescent white, deep shadow pockets.
Palette: Cold steel silver, toxic fluorescent green, matte black shadow tones.
Location: Claustrophobic commercial kitchen prep line at night.
Wardrobe: John wearing a stained white chef's coat, rolled sleeves, grey apron; Rosa wearing a distressed black denim jacket over a faded band tee.
Styling: John has sweaty, messy shoulder-length hair; Rosa has smudged eyeliner, damp dark hair with yellow highlights.
Sound: Low fridge hum, rhythmic dripping pipe, clattering metal. No music.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
WS, low angle. A narrow stainless-steel kitchen aisle. John stands frame-right hunched over a prep table; Rosa stands frame-left clutching a black clipboard, illuminated by harsh overhead light.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] ECU, TILT DOWN -> [ACT] Leaky steel pipe drips dirty water onto a stack of unpaid paper invoices -> [SFX] Slow, wet dripping, heavy fridge hum
[CAM] MS, tracking Rosa's movement -> [ACT] Rosa slams the black clipboard onto the steel counter, stepping forward -> Rosa: "We didn't order this! It's unpaid!" -> [SFX] Loud metallic slam
[CAM] MS, OTS over Rosa's shoulder -> [ACT] John jerks his head up, eyes bloodshot, jaw clenched in rage -> John: "We needed inventory! He knew that!" -> [SFX] Heavy, ragged male breathing
[CAM] MCU, handheld on Rosa -> [ACT] Rosa steps closer, pointing an angry finger at him, eyes welling with tears -> Rosa: "He was dying, John. And you just kept buying!" -> [SFX] Fluorescent light buzzing
[CAM] CU, quick whip pan to John -> [ACT] John grabs a wire whisk, his knuckles turning white, hand shaking -> John: "You weren't here! You left him to me!" -> [SFX] Whisk scraping steel counter
[CAM] MCU, OTS over John's shoulder -> [ACT] Rosa flinches, her angry expression breaking into deep grief, arms crossing tightly -> Rosa: "Because of you." -> [SFX] Dripping pipe continues
[CAM] MS, profile tracking shot -> [ACT] John hurls the wire whisk across the kitchen floor in frustration -> [SFX] Loud, chaotic metal clattering; John exhales a choked sob
[CAM] MCU, tracking Rosa as she backs away -> [ACT] Rosa shakes her head, wiping a tear with her sleeve, turning to leave -> Rosa (O.S.): "Look at this place. We're done." -> [SFX] Squeak of shoes on tile
[</BRIEF>]

IX. SURFACE TENSION

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a tense domestic drama film scene featuring @SARAH as SARAH (stepmother), @ROSA as ROSA (rebellious daughter), and @JOHN as JOHN (protective brother) at a fraught Sunday dinner.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Maintain strict 180-degree table axis. Sarah sits at the head, facing right. John and Rosa sit side-by-side, facing left. Keep Sarah’s serving bowl and John’s protective arm posture physically consistent across cuts.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Cold, minimalist domestic drama.
Lighting: Harsh, low-hanging overhead dining pendant casting heavy shadows. Cool daylight spills from a side window.
Palette: Desaturated teals, slate grays, cold wood tones.
Wardrobe: Sarah wears a beige silk blouse. Rosa is in a dark green denim jacket over a black t-shirt. John wears a heavy charcoal knit sweater.
Styling: Sarah has a neat, low bun. Rosa has a messy bob with bright yellow streaks. John has loose, wavy collar-length hair.
Sound: Foley and ambient only. No music. Tense, quiet room tone, sharp silverware clinks, and heavy breathing.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
MCU of Sarah holding a steaming white ceramic bowl of potatoes with both hands over the dark wood table, her strained, pleading smile fixed toward frame-right.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MCU, static, centered on Sarah holding the steaming bowl -> [ACT] Sarah looks off-camera right, her smile tight and desperate -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "I thought we could all go to the lake house this weekend. For Rosa's birthday." SFX: Quiet hiss of rising steam.
[CAM] INSERT SHOT, high-angle tight CU on Rosa's ceramic plate -> [ACT] A silver fork aggressively scrapes and stabs at a potato, leaving streaks -> [AUDIO] Rosa (O.S.): "I’d rather choke." SFX: Sharp screech of metal on porcelain.
[CAM] MCU, low-angle, looking slightly up at John and Rosa -> [ACT] John sits tense, his left arm angled in front of Rosa defensively. He stares coldly frame-left, his jaw muscles clenching -> [AUDIO] John: "She has exams, Sarah. We're busy." SFX: Low, tense breathing.
[CAM] OTS over John's heavy shoulder, looking at Sarah -> [ACT] Sarah’s smile slowly drops. She slowly lowers the heavy ceramic bowl onto the table -> [AUDIO] Sarah: "I'm only trying to help, John..." SFX: Dull clunk of the bowl hitting wood.
[CAM] CU of Rosa, camera-right of the axis -> [ACT] Rosa keeps her head down, avoiding eye contact, her fingers nervously picking at the cuff of her green denim jacket -> [AUDIO] Sarah (O.S.): "...I’m allowed to try."
[CAM] WIDE PROFILE, static lock-off showing the entire length of the table -> [ACT] Sarah sits isolated on the left, looking down at her hands. John and Rosa sit close together on the right, frozen in rigid, hostile silence -> [AUDIO] Cold, dead room tone.
[</BRIEF>]

X. THE OLD GUARD

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a dramatic scene featuring @ROB as ROBERT, @JOHN as JOHN, and @EVAN as EVAN, capturing a tense, grief-laden family struggle in a cold, naturalistic autumn setting.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Ensure spatial and prop continuity of the heavy oak dresser between shots. Maintain the 180-degree axis along the steep driveway line. Keep the movement of the heavy dresser slow, halting, and weighted to preserve physical realism.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Raw, bleak Scandinavian realist drama, heavy film grain, desaturated color grading.
Palette: Muted slate grays, cold overcast blues, dark pine greens, weathered wood browns.
Lighting: Diffused, high-contrast overcast daylight, casting cold overhead shadows on tired faces.
Location: A steep, winding gray-gravel driveway of a rustic cabin, bordered by bare skeletal trees.
Sound: Foley and ambient sound only. Piercing wind howling through bare branches, heavy breathing, sharp boots sliding on loose gravel, creaking wood. No music.
Robert: Windswept curly salt-and-pepper hair, red-rimmed eyes, dry lips. Wears a dark olive padded corduroy jacket with a sherpa collar and brown leather gloves.
John: Shoulder-length wavy light brown hair whipped across his forehead, jaw tensed. Wears a navy waxed-canvas chore coat and mud-caked steel-toed boots.
Evan: Dark parted fringe damp with sweat, red nose from the biting cold. Wears a distressed mustard-yellow canvas jacket and a thick black wool knit cap.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
MLS, low-angle looking up the steep, gravel driveway. John and Evan are mid-strain, carrying a massive dark oak dresser down the slope. John, at the front-lower end, walks backward, body tensed. Evan, at the upper end, faces forward, leaning low. Robert stands anxious in the background-right, hovering helplessly.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MLS, low-angle, slow tracking backward down the gravel slope -> [ACT] John struggles to maintain his grip, his arms shaking violently under the load as his boots slip on loose stones -> [AUDIO] Cold wind whistling, heavy grunts -> [DIALOGUE] JOHN: (grunting, arms shaking violently) "Evan... lift your side. It’s dragging."
[CAM] CUT TO: MCU, tracking side-view focusing on Evan -> [ACT] Evan grits his teeth, his fingers slipping slightly on the polished dark oak edge of the dresser as he desperately tries to find footing on the shifting gray gravel -> [AUDIO] Crushing gravel, sharp gasp -> [DIALOGUE] EVAN: (panting, teeth grit) "I’m trying... the gravel is... sliding under my boots." -> [DIALOGUE] ROBERT (O.S.): (voice shaky and desperate) "Let me take the corner, boys, just—"
[CAM] CUT TO: MCU, OTS looking over the corner of the heavy oak dresser toward Robert -> [ACT] Robert steps forward into frame-left, his gloved hands raised toward the dresser. In frame-right, John’s shoulders tense, keeping his head down and refusing to look up -> [AUDIO] Gust of cold wind, fabric rustling -> [DIALOGUE] JOHN: (curt, eyes locked on the ground) "No, Dad. Stay back. You’ll fall."
[CAM] CUT TO: MS, static wide-angle shot from the bottom of the slope looking up -> [ACT] John and Evan slowly carry the heavy dresser down and past the camera, exiting frame-left. Robert remains standing completely still in the center of the frame, arms hanging limp, looking down at the gravel as the cold autumn wind whips around his jacket. -> [AUDIO] Slowly receding crunching footsteps on wet gravel, deep heavy sigh, howling wind.
[</BRIEF>]

XI. VIGIL

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a cinematic dramatic scene featuring @ROB as ROBERT and @SARAH as SARAH, capturing empty-nest estrangement and unspoken tension in a realist style.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Ensure tight spatial continuity across the kitchen island axis. Maintain Robert’s fixed position frame-right by the window and Sarah's frame-left position at the island. Match the progression of the kitchen towel folding across cuts.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Visual Style: Scandi-minimalist kitchen, cold walnut cabinetry, white marble countertops, clean geometric lines, empty spaces.
Lighting: Cool, dim volumetric blue night light through the window; a single warm, low-hanging pendant lamp casting sharp shadows over the island.
Color Palette: Dark charcoal, slate blue, warm amber, stark white.
Robert Styling: Wavy salt-and-pepper hair swept back, light stubble; charcoal-grey wool knit sweater.
Sarah Styling: Straight ash-blonde hair tied in a loose, low ponytail with loose strands; makeup-free look with tired, pale eyes; oversized beige cashmere cardigan.
Props: Crisp white linen kitchen towel.
Music/Sound: No Music. Diegetic Only. Persistent low hum of a refrigerator, dead room tone, a single melancholic, low-register cello note fading in late in the scene.
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
EXTREME CLOSE-UP on a white linen kitchen towel being folded with geometric precision on a white marble countertop, lit by a pool of amber light. In the soft-focus background, Robert's dark silhouette stands motionless by a window.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] ECU, stationary lockoff on the marble surface -> [ACT] Sarah’s slender fingers precisely crease the edge of the linen towel, adjusting a corner -> [AUDIO] Low hum of the refrigerator. SARAH: "She promised she’d text when they passed the state line."
[CAM] MCU, low-angle looking up at Robert standing frame-right, his face illuminated by the cold blue light reflecting off the window glass -> [ACT] Robert continues staring out at the dark, empty driveway, his throat clearing softly -> [AUDIO] ROBERT: "It’s a long drive. Dark, too."
[CAM] Tight OTS from behind Robert's shoulder, looking across the vast kitchen gap at Sarah standing at the island -> [ACT] Sarah abruptly freezes her folding motion, her palms remaining flat on the half-folded towel -> [AUDIO] SARAH: "It is."
[CAM] High-angle extreme wide shot, looking straight down at the stark, immaculate kitchen island separating them -> [ACT] Both remain completely motionless on opposite sides of the frame -> [AUDIO] SARAH (O.S.): "Very dark."
[CAM] CU, slightly high-angle looking down at Sarah’s face -> [ACT] She stares downward at her hands, her jaw tightening as she swallows hard, refusing to look toward the window -> [AUDIO] Sustained low cello note begins to blend with the refrigerator hum.
[CAM] MCU on Robert, profile view by the window pane -> [ACT] Robert slowly turns his head away from the glass, looking toward Sarah frame-left, his expression heavy and exhausted -> [AUDIO] ROBERT: "...Are you hungry?"
[CAM] Low-angle MS from the end of the kitchen island, framing both of them in profile, separated by the empty marble counter -> [ACT] Robert slowly takes a step away from the window toward the wall panel, while Sarah remains frozen, staring at the towel -> [AUDIO] Heavy silence; the low cello note swells slightly as the hum of the refrigerator continues.
[</BRIEF>]

XII. AGAINST THE COLD

[[STAGING]]
[[INTENT]]
Create a indie coming-of-age drama scene featuring @JOHN as John and @ROSA as Rosa.
[[/INTENT]]

[[LOGIC]]
Preserve 180-degree axis across floor-seated blocking. Track letter prop in John's left hand and cardboard box on floor. Ensure match-on-action screen direction and continuous lighting.
[[/LOGIC]]

[[AESTHETIC]]
Style: Raw 35mm film, soft grain
Light: Low-key, cool moonlight through slatted window, warm floor lamp glow
Palette: Dusty amber, indigo shadows, faded pine
Set: Cluttered attic, low slanted ceiling, exposed beams, boxes, old toys
Sound: Floor creaks, heavy breathing, dry paper rustles, distant wind
Wardrobe: John wearing a dark gray wool sweater over a faded white t-shirt; Rosa wearing an oversized, black corduroy button-down shirt over a dark green ribbed tank top
[[/AESTHETIC]]

[[OPENING]]
EST. High angle wide shot of the dusty attic floor. John sits cross-legged frame-right next to an open cardboard box, holding a faded yellow envelope. Rosa sits opposite him frame-left, knees hugged to her chest.
[[/OPENING]]
[[/STAGING]]

[<BRIEF>]
[CAM] MCU, slow dolly in on Rosa looking down, defensive posture -> [ACT] Rosa lets out a dry, bitter half-laugh, shaking her head -> [AUDIO] Rosa: "We actually believed those promises?"
[CAM] OTS over Rosa's shoulder tracking John holding the aged paper in both hands -> [ACT] John unfolds the paper carefully, eyes scanning the handwritten lines -> [AUDIO] Rosa (O.S.): "He didn't care. Not then, not today."
[CAM] CU of John's face, warm light hitting his cheek, soft expression -> [ACT] John looks up from the letter to Rosa, eyes moist but firm -> [AUDIO] John: "Listen to this part. 'I'll build the fort high...'"
[CAM] Low angle CU of Rosa's face, tight jaw, eyes darting up to meet his gaze -> [ACT] Rosa's eyes soften slightly, a tear escaping down her cheek, her posture tense -> [AUDIO] John (O.S.): "...'so the cold can't find us.'"
[CAM] MCU, OTS over John's shoulder tracking Rosa's reaction -> [ACT] Rosa unclasps her knees, leaning forward slightly, swallowing hard -> [AUDIO] Rosa: "He wrote that?" -> John (O.S.): "You did."
[CAM] INS, low angle extreme close-up of John's hands gently smoothing down the creased edge of the old letter -> [ACT] John's thumb runs over a childish crayon drawing of two stick figures on the dusty floorboards -> [AUDIO] Faint paper rustle, distant wind rattling the wooden siding outside
[CAM] MCU, John looking directly at Rosa, tilting his head slightly -> [ACT] John offers a small, melancholic smile, extending his right hand slightly toward her -> [AUDIO] John: "We kept it, Rose. We're still here."
[CAM] Tight MCU on Rosa, looking down at his hand, then up at his eyes -> [ACT] Rosa takes a deep, shaky breath, her shoulders dropping as the bitterness drains from her face -> [AUDIO] Rosa: "We are."
[CAM] Wide profile shot tracking both siblings sitting on the floor -> [ACT] Rosa reaches out, her hand resting over John's on the dusty wood. They sit together in silence, illuminated by the soft amber lamplight -> [ENV] Ambient hum of the old attic, shadows stretching across the wooden ceiling
[</BRIEF>]

cues.selectedText: Low angle, slow push
Robert stands frame-left over the chopping block, Evan stands frame-right
Robert struggles to hoist a massive pine log, his shoulders trembling
Cold mist rising from the wet ground.
OTS tracking shot over Robert’s shoulder
Evan steps forward, hands open and welcoming
Let me, Dad.
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: ECU, shallow depth of field
Robert's hands in worn leather gloves
Robert's gloved fingers dig violently into the rough, wet bark
You've been at it for hours.
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: MCU, low-angle on Robert
Sweat on Robert's pale brow, labored breath
Robert glares downward, gripping the log tight to his chest
If you don't stack it right, the bottom rots.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: Tight CU on Evan
Evan's soft eyes hardening
Evan swallows hard, jaw tightening in a silent moment of realization
I won't be here next year.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: MCU, profile shot holding both in frame
Evan steps closer but keeps his hands at his sides, refusing to touch the wood
Then teach me.
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: Wide shot from ground level
Robert stares at the log, his shoulders slumping as he slowly lets it drop to the damp earth with a heavy thud
I'm trying.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: Tilt-down close shot
The log resting in wet pine needles
The wind gently rustles the dry leaves around the wood
MCU, tracking backward ahead of Sarah, keeping her centered
Sarah walks fast, hands clutching her beige woolen coat tight to her chest. Lea maintains a protective grip on Sarah’s arm. Rosa steps up on the left side, eyes wild with panic
Traffic roar, wet footsteps.
We can't just... accept that. There have to be other trials.
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: OTS MCU over Sarah's left shoulder, looking back slightly at Lea and Rosa walking in lockstep
Lea looks sharply at Rosa, shaking her head with deep urgency, her grip tightening on Sarah’s arm. Rosa looks back desperately
Rain hissing on asphalt.
Rosa, stop. Not here. Not on the street.
cues.speaker: Lea

cues.selectedText: MS side tracking shot, profiling all three moving left-to-right past glowing neon shop windows
Sarah keeps staring straight ahead with glazed, wet eyes, her jaw clenched. Rosa lunges slightly forward to catch Sarah's eye, gesturing with her hand
Distant police siren.
I just... I want to get to the car.
cues.speaker: Sarah

cues.selectedText: Mom, please, look at me—
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: MCU tracking backward, centered on Sarah's face

Sarah ignores the plea, staring blankly into the cold air as a tear spills down her cheek. Lea keeps her head down, walking forward firmly. Rosa slowly falls half a step back, her hand dropping in defeat
Heavy whoosh of a passing city bus, wet tire spray, three pairs of boots hitting concrete, rapid breathing.
CU, slow DOLLY IN on spinning tape reel
Dust particles kick off the rotating magnetic ribbon as a distorted, low female voice speaks through static
Tape hiss, muffled female voice
...we had that summer...
cues.speaker: muffled female voice

cues.selectedText: MS, TILT UP from a stack of moldy tape boxes to John standing frame-right, staring down
John rubs his temples with his right hand, his shoulders tense and hunched, breathing heavily
That wasn't July, Dad. She was already too sick.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: OTS over John's shoulder to Robert at the console, frame-left
Robert keeps his left hand resting on a fader, adjusting it sharply, eyes fixed on the VU meter needle leaping into the red
I mixed this session myself. I know the date, John.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: MCU, PAN L to R following John as he steps closer to the console
John shakes his head, teeth clenched, pointing a finger at the spinning reel
You weren't even there! You were in Chicago!
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: CU, tight on Robert, warm amber light hitting his weathered cheek
Robert flinches, his jaw tightening, eyes glistening with sudden tears as he glares at John off-screen
Don't you dare tell me where I was.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: OTS over Robert's shoulder focusing on John, frame-right
John's defensive posture crumbles; his jaw slackens and he lets out a shaky exhale
We both left her alone.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: MS, wide tracking shot circling slowly around both men standing side-by-side
Robert slowly takes his hand off the console slider, letting his arm drop to his side, staring blankly ahead
Tape hiss continues, mother's distorted voice (O.S.)
...always together...
cues.speaker: mother's distorted voice

cues.selectedText: CU on John's hand slowly reaching out and gripping the cold metal edge of the mixing board
His knuckles turn white as he suppresses a sob, head bowed, long hair falling forward
ECU, locked-off shot of the tape reel suddenly stopping
The magnetic tape ends and flaps loosely against the plastic spindle, spinning freely in the dim amber light
Steady, monotonous click-click of the loose tape, background dripping water
MCU, low-angle looking up slightly at Evan, frame-right
Evan stands frozen, clutching the receipt in his left hand, his eyes wide and glassy with unshed tears as his jaw tenses
Hey. You... you look good, Lea.
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: OTS over Evan's shoulder, tight on Lea frame-left
Lea fumbles a small white box of cold medicine in her trembling fingers, her eyes downcast before darting up to meet his
Thanks. I’m just... getting some things.
cues.speaker: Lea

cues.selectedText: CU, side profile, shallow depth of field focusing on Evan's twitching right hand
Evan steps forward slightly into the light, his empty right hand twitching in a half-gesture toward her before dropping
I wanted to... I never called because—
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: MCU, low-angle on Lea, camera-left of axis
Lea shakes her head frantically, her lips trembling as she clutches the medicine box tightly to her chest and takes a step back
Don't. Please. There's nothing... we don't have to.
cues.speaker: Lea

cues.selectedText: ECU, tight on Evan's eyes, glistening in the harsh white light
Evan's gaze drops slightly, his throat muscles moving as he swallows, his expression utterly defeated
My heart is broken, Lea. It's still...
cues.speaker: Evan

cues.selectedText: CU on Lea, side profile
Lea looks away toward the cold metal shelf, biting her bottom lip hard to suppress a sob, a single tear escaping her eye
Shaky, shallow inhale, distant hum of the pharmacy refrigerator.
Wide-angle, low down on the dirty linoleum floor, looking down the narrow aisle
Lea takes a hurried, shaky step past Evan, her heavy boots squeaking on the floor, leaving him standing motionless
Squeak of boots on linoleum, rustle of her heavy winter parka.
MS, handheld, drift-left
John grips the box, knuckles white
We can't keep everything, Dad. Just pick.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Cardboard scraping.
MCU, low-angle on Robert near window
Robert points an accusatory finger, chest heaving
That's my life in those boxes!
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: OTS behind Robert looking at John
John steps closer, jaw tight with resentment
You didn't care about this house for ten years!
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: CU, tracking Robert
Robert opens his mouth to yell, but freezes. His pointing hand stays suspended, his angry gaze suddenly shifting to a hollow, terrifying blankness
INS, close-up on the box
John's grip on the cardboard box loosens, a vinyl sleeve sliding outward slightly
Paper rustling.
MCU, OTS behind Robert's frozen shoulder
John's anger instantly drains, replaced by suffocating shock, his lips parting
Dad...?
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: CU on Robert
Robert's hand slowly drops to his side. He blinks rapidly, looking around the room as if seeing it for the first time
Where... where is your mother?
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: MS, profile tracking
John slowly, silently lowers the heavy box onto a wooden table frame-left, keeping his eyes locked on his father
She's not here, Dad.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Dull thud of cardboard on wood.
WS, extreme low-angle
Thick dust motes drift lazily through a bright shaft of sunlight cutting between the two men
Distant wind.
MCU, slow push-in on John
John's eyes well with tears. He swallows hard, staring at his father's vacant silhouette in the foreground
Low-angle, wide shot looking up from floor-level. The frame is horizontally bisected by a large, taut white bedsheet held tight
Sarah stands frame-left, eyes locked forward with intense focus. John stands frame-right, holding his end with a white-knuckled grip
Deep dryer rumble, sharp rustle of fabric.
Don't drag the corner on the floor, John.
cues.speaker: Sarah

cues.selectedText: High-angle close-up looking down at John's hands gripping the corners of the sheet
John sighs heavily, rolling his eyes as he slightly drops his wrists, loosening his side of the fabric
John's heavy sigh, dry friction of cotton.
It’s clean, Mom. It doesn't matter.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Extreme close-up of Sarah’s face in profile, her jaw tightening as she stares forward
She pulls her end of the sheet back sharply to restore the tension
High-pitched creak of the wooden floorboards under her feet.
It matters to me...
cues.speaker: Sarah

cues.selectedText: ...We keep things clean.
cues.speaker: Sarah

cues.selectedText: Medium close-up, OTS looking past Sarah's shoulder at John
John's eyes narrow in sudden, bitter anger. He jerks his arms back hard, snapping the sheet taut with a violent pop
Loud, whip-like snap of the bedsheet.
Is that why you cleared out his closet already? To keep things 'clean'?
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Close-up on Sarah's face
Her eyes widen in shock. Her lips part slightly as she gasps, her fingers instantly losing their grip and letting go of the fabric
Short, sharp intake of breath.
That’s... that is not fair.
cues.speaker: Sarah

cues.selectedText: Dutch-tilt ECU on the rusted socket wrench
Robert’s thumb slowly strokes the cold metal
Low ambient hum, faint metallic scraping.
Low-angle OTS looking past Robert's shoulder toward John in the background
Robert stands rigid at the cluttered wooden workbench. John is hunched over a pile of cardboard boxes, back turned
Your grandfather bought this in '72.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: Still works.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: High-angle extreme close-up on John's hands
John violently pulls a strip of brown packing tape off a squealing dispenser
Sharp, piercing screech of tearing tape.
It’s trash, Dad.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Profile MCU of John, camera tracking slightly as he works
John keeps his head down, jaw clenched, avoiding eye contact as he smooths the tape with a hard swipe
Let it go.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: Low-angle CU on Robert's face, dusty light highlighting his weathered features
Robert's chin trembles slightly, his gaze fixed on the wrench in his right hand
Some things...
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: ECU on the tape dispenser
John's hands suddenly freeze mid-motion, his fingers squeezing the plastic handle until knuckles turn white
Sudden, heavy silence.
...they don't lose value just because they're old, John.
cues.speaker: Robert

cues.selectedText: Extreme low-angle OTS looking up from the box floor to John's face
John slowly tilts his head up, eyes cold, staring intensely across the garage at his father
Then you should have kept better care of them.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: ECU, TILT DOWN
Leaky steel pipe drips dirty water onto a stack of unpaid paper invoices
Slow, wet dripping, heavy fridge hum
MS, tracking Rosa's movement
Rosa slams the black clipboard onto the steel counter, stepping forward
Loud metallic slam
We didn't order this! It's unpaid!
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: MS, OTS over Rosa's shoulder
John jerks his head up, eyes bloodshot, jaw clenched in rage
Heavy, ragged male breathing
We needed inventory. He knew that.
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: MCU, handheld on Rosa
Rosa steps closer, pointing an angry finger at him, eyes welling with tears
Fluorescent light buzzing
He was dying, John.
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: And you just kept buying.
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: CU, quick whip pan to John
John grabs a wire whisk, his knuckles turning white, hand shaking
Whisk scraping steel counter
You weren't here. You left him to me!
cues.speaker: John

cues.selectedText: MCU, OTS over John's shoulder
Rosa flinches, her angry expression breaking into deep grief, arms crossing tightly
Dripping pipe continues
Because of you.

cues.selectedText: MS, profile tracking shot
John hurls the wire whisk across the kitchen floor in frustration
Loud, chaotic metal clattering; John exhales a choked sob

Look at this place. We're done.

Sarah looks off-camera right, her smile tight and desperate

I thought we could all go to the lake house this weekend. For Rosa's birthday.

We are.
cues.speaker: Rosa

cues.selectedText: Wide profile shot tracking both siblings sitting on the floor
Rosa reaches out, her hand resting over John's on the dusty wood. They sit together in silence, illuminated by the soft amber lamplight
Ambient hum of the old attic, shadows stretching across the wooden ceiling
